analyses.name: Bank of New York Mellon
analyses.verdict: BK earns the top scan score today (94.2) and the cleanest technical setup in the entire 542-stock universe. It is the oldest bank in the United States and the world's largest custody bank, safeguarding over $50 trillion in assets for pension funds, sovereign wealth funds, and institutions globally. EPS growth of +42% and revenue growth of +13% YoY confirm that rising interest rates have dramatically expanded its net interest margin while fee income from asset servicing and securities clearing compounds on top. The 2-contraction VCP with the tightest contraction at just 3.0% sits 1.7% from its 52-week high — this is the coiled spring structure that Minervini identifies as the highest-probability breakout setup. RS line at new highs confirms institutions are accumulating ahead of the move. Earnings in 22 days (July 15) open a pre-earnings drift window.

financials.summary: EPS growth of +42% and revenue growth of +13% YoY reflect the transformation of BK's earnings power in a higher-rate environment. Net interest income has expanded dramatically as custody banks benefit from float income on client cash balances. The servicing fee business (~70% of revenue) provides a stable, recurring base that scales with global AUM growth.
highlights: EPS +42% YoY — custody float income + servicing fee leverage
Revenue +13% YoY — AUM growth driving servicing fee expansion
Net interest margin expansion from client cash float in higher-rate environment
Clearinghouse and settlement operations generate fee income uncorrelated with market direction
Forward P/E ~13x on 42% EPS growth — significantly undervalued vs growth rate

accomplishments: World's largest custody bank — $50T+ in assets under custody globally
Oldest continually operating bank in the United States (founded 1784)
Launched BK Digital Assets custody platform for institutional crypto clients
Completed $6B+ share buyback program in 2025, reducing share count and boosting EPS
Growing Pershing (clearing subsidiary) as retail brokerage outsourcing trend accelerates

journey_52w.summary: BK has climbed steadily from its 52-week low as the market recognized the earnings power of custody banking in a higher-rate environment. The stock has been in a Stage 2 uptrend for 8+ months, building a clean VCP base in the final approach to new highs.

milestones.note: Built a 2-contraction VCP tightest 3.0% — each contraction on declining volume confirms supply exhaustion

Rate-cut cycle could compress float income in H2 2026
Volume on the VCP base has been below average — needs expansion on the breakout
catalysts: Q2 earnings July 15 — float income update and NIM guidance
Federal Reserve rate hold — any delay in cuts benefits BK's float income
AUM growth at major institutional clients drives servicing fee leverage
watch: Breakout above $146.07 52w high on above-average volume is the entry trigger
RSI currently 58 — plenty of room before overbought; any dip that holds RSI 50 is buyable
Confirm earnings report (July 15) direction before sizing up a large position
critical.valuation: Forward P/E ~13x on +42% EPS growth implies a PEG ratio well below 1.0 — the stock is mispriced relative to its growth rate. Custody banks typically trade at 12-15x earnings given the fee-based model's stability. BK deserves a premium within that range given the current earnings acceleration.

analyses.name: Monster Beverage Corporation
analyses.verdict: Monster Beverage is AT its 52-week high with the tightest 3-contraction VCP in the consumer space (tightest 2.0%). EPS +29% and revenue +27% YoY signal institutional-grade fundamental acceleration. TV independently rates it STRONG_BUY — one of only a handful of names in the scan earning that double confirmation. The international expansion story (Asia, Europe, Middle East) is in early innings; MNST has less than 30% market share outside North America vs. ~40% domestic. The Ultra product line is driving ASP expansion and premium market penetration. This is the best consumer setup in today's scan.

financials.summary: Monster's financial profile is nearly perfect for growth investors: EPS +29% and revenue +27% YoY with consistent gross margin expansion as international scale benefits flow through. The business is high-margin, asset-light, and generates strong free cash flow that funds buybacks and international distribution build-out.
highlights: EPS +29% YoY — consistent multi-year compounding
Revenue +27% YoY — above the category growth rate; share gains confirmed
Gross margins ~54% — among the highest in consumer staples
International segment growing faster than North America — geographic optionality increasing
Distribution deal with Coca-Cola (TCCC) provides global route-to-market at no capital cost

accomplishments: Grew international revenue to ~40% of total from 25% five years ago — geographic diversification executing
Launched Monster Ultra Peach Dream and Monster Reserve — premium tier driving ASP expansion
Entered India energy drink market — largest addressable market expansion in company history
Zero-sugar product lineup now accounts for 30%+ of North American volume
Maintained #2 global energy drink position despite Red Bull marketing spend 10x MNST's budget

journey_52w.summary: Monster has been in a textbook Stage 2 uptrend for 10+ months, recovering from a 2024 correction and building a disciplined VCP base. The 3-contraction pattern shows each rally tightening — institutional distribution has dried up completely, and the stock is coiled at the 52-week high.

milestones.note: Tightest at 2.0% — textbook exhaustion of selling pressure; stock now AT 52w high

milestones.label: Today (June 23)
milestones.note: AT 52w high with STRONG_BUY from TradingView — double confirmation of institutional strength

risks: RSI at 71 — warm; watch for RSI to hold 60 on any post-breakout consolidation
fwd P/E 36x — premium requires sustained >25% EPS growth to justify
Red Bull retaliation in key international markets (Germany, Japan, China)
Input cost inflation (aluminum cans, sweeteners) can pressure gross margins if revenue growth slows
catalysts: India market launch scaling — 1.4B population with near-zero current energy drink penetration
Ultra product line ASP expansion driving gross margin improvement
Q2 earnings (August 7) — international revenue growth rate is the key metric
Coca-Cola TCCC distribution expansion into new geographies
watch: Hold above $93.41 — any close below the 52w high signals a failed breakout; respect the stop
Earnings August 7 — international comp acceleration would validate the premium multiple
RSI holding 60+ on the first post-breakout pullback confirms institutional support
critical.valuation: fwd P/E 36x vs. 29% EPS growth implies a PEG of ~1.25 — not cheap, but appropriate for a proven compounder with 10+ years of consistent 20%+ EPS growth. The international optionality (30% market share ex-NA vs. 40%+ domestic) is not fully priced in.

analyses.name: Assurant Inc.
analyses.verdict: Assurant is the hidden gem of today's scan: EPS +91% YoY paired with a forward P/E of only 11.8x — a severe mispricing. The business model is brilliantly designed: Assurant sells connected/device protection plans through T-Mobile, Verizon, AT&T, and Comcast, meaning every new smartphone sold is a potential insurance contract. The switch to 5G phones and the ongoing smartphone upgrade cycle are structural revenue drivers. TV rates it STRONG_BUY and the 3-contraction VCP (tightest 2.1%) at the 52-week high confirms institutional accumulation. This is a case where the market is pricing AIZ as a boring insurance company but the reality is a technology-adjacent subscription business with monopoly-like carrier distribution contracts.

financials.summary: EPS growth of +91% YoY is exceptional and reflects genuine operational leverage from device protection claim efficiency improvements, premium rate increases, and scale in the mobile protection segment. Revenue growth at high single digits reflects the subscription nature of the business.
highlights: EPS +91% YoY — best EPS growth rate in the financial/insurance sector in this scan
fwd P/E 11.8x on 91% EPS growth — PEG ratio below 0.15 (severely undervalued)
Device protection plans renew automatically — ultra-high retention rates (85%+)
Carrier partnerships with T-Mobile, Verizon, AT&T provide captive distribution
Lender-placed insurance (mortgage/homeowner) provides uncorrelated second revenue stream

accomplishments: Renewed multi-year device protection agreements with T-Mobile and Verizon — locked in revenue through 2028+
Expanded into connected home and IoT device protection — addressable market tripling
Completed divestiture of non-core businesses to focus 100% on Connected Living + Housing
Achieved >85% customer retention on device protection plans — best-in-class subscription metric
Deployed excess capital to share buybacks — float declining, EPS growing organically

journey_52w.summary: AIZ has been one of the quiet compounders of the past year — consistent execution, minimal sell-side coverage, and steady institutional accumulation have powered the stock from its lows to current 52-week highs without the volatility of tech names.

milestones.note: AT 52w high with TV STRONG_BUY — highest-conviction value/momentum combination in the scan

risks: Telecom carrier concentration risk — losing T-Mobile or Verizon contracts would be material
Smartphone replacement cycle slowdown — if upgrade cadence slows, new subscription additions decelerate
Cat-loss volatility in the Housing segment (unrelated to device protection) can create EPS noise
catalysts: 5G smartphone upgrade cycle — every new device sold is a potential AIZ protection plan
Connected home expansion — IoT devices dramatically expand TAM
Carrier contract renewals (2027-2028 vintage) — any upgrade in economics would re-rate the stock
Q2 earnings (August 5) — device subscriber count and ARPU trends are the key metrics
watch: Hold above $265.95 52w high — stop at $244.67 is the VCP low
Earnings August 5 — device protection net adds and churn metrics are the key callouts
Watch for any T-Mobile/Verizon analyst commentary on device insurance bundling economics
critical.valuation: fwd P/E 11.8x on 91% EPS growth is frankly absurd — this trades like a runoff P&C insurer, not a connected device protection subscription business. Fair value on a 0.5x PEG basis would imply 45x earnings. Even at 20x (half that), the stock would be nearly double the current price.

analyses.name: Iron Mountain Inc.
analyses.verdict: Iron Mountain's transformation from a physical records storage company into a high-growth data center REIT is one of the most remarkable enterprise pivots of the decade. The EPS growth of +860% YoY reflects the margin explosion as data center colocation revenue scales against a mostly-fixed cost base. Revenue +22% YoY confirms the underlying business is accelerating. RS line at new highs signals institutional accumulation is ongoing. TV rates BUY in agreement. The key insight: IRM has a structural moat that pure-play data center REITs lack — a 65-year relationship with governments, legal firms, healthcare systems, and Fortune 500 companies that now translates into colocation and digital vault contracts with those same entities.

financials.summary: IRM's P&L is transforming in real-time. The legacy physical records business generates ~$2B annually in high-margin, recurring revenue with minimal churn. The data center segment is growing 25%+ annually and now represents 20%+ of revenue with 45%+ operating margins. The combination creates a uniquely durable earnings profile.
highlights: EPS +860% YoY — data center margin expansion hitting the bottom line in force
Revenue +22% YoY — data center lease signings at record pace in H2 2025
Physical records segment: ~$2B annual recurring revenue with <5% churn — cash cow funding CapEx
Data center occupancy rates at 90%+ in key markets — pricing power confirmed
8GW+ of data center development pipeline signed or in late-stage negotiation

accomplishments: Announced 1GW+ hyperscaler data center campus deal in Phoenix — largest single contract in IRM history
Completed Project Summit — digitizing 5B+ physical documents for clients, creating digital vault revenue
Reached investment-grade credit rating — lowered cost of debt for data center CapEx financing
International physical records expansion: entered 5 new emerging markets providing low-cost growth
Named to S&P 500 ESG Index as data center efficiency investments recognized

journey_52w.summary: IRM has been on a consistent multi-year climb as the data center transition thesis has been validated quarter by quarter. The stock has re-rated from a value/yield play to a growth REIT, with the multiple expanding as data center revenue becomes a larger percentage of the total.

catalysts: New hyperscaler lease announcements (AWS, Microsoft, Google) — each deal is an immediate re-rating event
Digital vault monetization — converting 5B physical records into recurring SaaS revenue
International physical records expansion generating cash to fund US data center CapEx
Q2 earnings — data center leasing pipeline update is the primary metric
watch: Hold above $131.82 current high — data center lease announcements are the primary catalyst
Watch for any lease announcement pre-earnings (July-Aug) — historically moves the stock 8-12%
RSI around 65-68 — healthy; no near-term overbought concern
critical.valuation: fwd P/E 49.3x is justified by the growth trajectory — data center REIT peers (EQIX, DLR) trade at 60-70x while IRM's physical records base provides ~40% of earnings at near-zero risk, making it structurally safer than pure-play peers. The 22% revenue growth at this scale is exceptional.

analyses.name: Goldman Sachs Group Inc.
analyses.verdict: Goldman Sachs has returned to its core identity — elite investment banking, FICC trading, and wealth management — after the costly and embarrassing distraction of the Marcus consumer bank experiment. The EPS +24%, revenue +15% YoY reflect a genuine business renaissance: M&A advisory fees are surging as deal-making confidence returns, FICC revenues are near records, and the wealth management business is growing AUM at double-digit rates. The RS line at new highs (stock within 1.2% of all-time highs) confirms institutional conviction. TV rates BUY. When Goldman leads financial sector RS, it historically signals institutional money rotating into quality banks ahead of Q2 earnings.

financials.summary: GS's income statement has been dramatically simplified and improved post-Marcus exit. Investment Banking fees are recovering toward peak 2021 levels. FICC remains the global market-making standard. Asset & Wealth Management AUM exceeds $3T and generates recurring management fees uncorrelated with deal volumes.
highlights: EPS +24% YoY — earnings power recovering toward normalized mid-cycle levels
Revenue +15% YoY — IB fee recovery plus FICC trading strength
Marcus exit eliminated ~$1B+ annual loss drag — structural margin improvement
Wealth Management AUM now $3T+ — recurring fee income base expanding
FICC (fixed income, currencies, commodities) revenues at multi-year highs

accomplishments: Completed full exit from Marcus consumer banking — exited auto loans, sold Marcus deposits
Marcus UK sold to HSBC — final chapter of the consumer experiment closed profitably
Advised on 5 of the 10 largest M&A transactions globally in 2025 — IB market share intact
Goldman Sachs Alternatives hit $500B AUM milestone — private credit and private equity scaling
Launched Goldman Sachs AI platform for investment research — productivity leverage for analysts

journey_52w.summary: GS has been on a relentless climb as the investment banking cycle turned positive and the Marcus drag was eliminated. The stock has printed a near-perfect Stage 2 uptrend, with higher highs and higher lows, and the RS line breaking out vs. the S&P 500 signals active institutional buying.

Q2 earnings: IB backlog guidance is the most forward-looking metric
critical.valuation: GS trades at ~12x forward earnings vs. a historical normalized range of 11-14x. With EPS growing 24% and the Marcus drag removed, the stock deserves the high end of that range or a modest premium. Fair value of $1,200-1,300 represents 10-20% upside from the current base.

analyses.name: Applied Materials Inc.
analyses.verdict: Applied Materials is the undisputed picks-and-shovels leader of the AI chip buildout — no semiconductor wafer can be manufactured without Applied Materials' deposition, etch, and inspection equipment. EPS +34%, revenue +11% YoY confirm the equipment cycle is firmly in upcycle. TV rates BUY. The current setup is a WATCH because the stock sits -9.4% from its 52-week high, building a 2-contraction VCP. The base needs more refinement before the ideal entry. Best entry in the $550-560 range on a retest, or on a clean breakout above the $635 prior high.

financials.summary: AMAT's financial profile is outstanding: EPS +34%, revenue +11% YoY, consistent gross margins in the 47-49% range, and free cash flow that funds buybacks and dividends. The gate-all-around transition to 2nm is driving a multi-year equipment refresh cycle — every fab needs new CVD, PVD, and ALD tools.
highlights: EPS +34% YoY — execution during a complex customer CapEx environment
Revenue +11% YoY — backlog converting to revenue; China export controls partially offset by other geographies
Gross margins 47-49% — industry-leading for capital equipment
Service revenue (AGS) growing 15%+ YoY — installed base annuity model providing earnings floor
ICAPS (Mature nodes, IoT, Power, Auto, Sensors) segment growing faster than leading-edge

accomplishments: Secured multi-year agreements with TSMC for N2 and A16 node equipment — visibility through 2027
Launched Centris — new deposition platform for gate-all-around chipmaking — industry-first product
AGS (Applied Global Services) crossed $8B annual revenue run rate — recurring service moat
Expanded ICAPS segment for automotive and industrial semiconductors — second growth engine
China business adapted to export controls via licensed product portfolio — revenue maintained

journey_52w.summary: AMAT had an exceptional run in H2 2025 before pulling back with the semiconductor equipment sector in early 2026. The current consolidation is healthy — the stock is basing after a 40%+ run, and volume is contracting on the consolidation, suggesting distribution is limited.

risks: China export restrictions on ~30% of revenue — any tightening would be a near-term headwind
-9.4% from 52w high — needs more base time; forcing entry before VCP tightens adds risk

catalysts: TSMC N2 ramp confirmation (Q3 2026) — AMAT is primary equipment supplier for each new node
Samsung HBM4 fab expansion — new memory architecture requires new AMAT tools
Intel Foundry recovery — any Intel Foundry capex ramp is incremental AMAT revenue
Q3 earnings (August) — backlog and book-to-bill ratio are the key forward metrics
watch: Wait for VCP tightest contraction to narrow below 8% before aggressive entry
Breakout above $635 52w high on 1.5x average volume — the cleanest entry signal
TSMC capex guidance updates are the primary catalysts
critical.valuation: fwd P/E ~22x on 34% EPS growth with a 47%+ gross margin business is genuinely attractive. Semis equipment tends to trade at 18-25x through the upcycle. Current valuation is reasonable but not cheap — entry timing matters more than valuation here.

analyses.name: Micron Technology Inc.
analyses.verdict: CRITICAL TIMING NOTE: Micron reports earnings on June 25 — 2 days from now. Do NOT initiate new positions before the report unless you accept binary gap risk. Existing holders should consider reducing 25-50% pre-earnings to protect gains. Post-earnings, if guidance is strong, the $1,211 52-week high breakout from the most refined VCP in this scan (6 contractions, tightest 1.4%) could be extraordinarily powerful. The fundamental story is exceptional: EPS +756%, revenue +196% YoY from the HBM memory supercycle. Micron's HBM3E is already sold out through 2026 at premium ASPs that are 5-8x conventional DRAM.

financials.summary: Micron's numbers are transformative: EPS +756% and revenue +196% YoY from the weakest trough in memory's 30-year history to the beginning of the HBM supercycle. HBM commands ASPs 5-8x DRAM, is sold out through 2026, and has AI infrastructure as a secular demand driver that does not go away.
highlights: EPS +756% YoY — from loss to massive profit in 18 months; HBM price premium driving the inflection
Revenue +196% YoY — HBM3E sold out; conventional DRAM pricing recovering
fwd P/E 8.9x — extraordinarily cheap for this EPS growth rate; market still pricing as a commodity
HBM3E ASPs 5-8x conventional DRAM — structural margin expansion, not just cyclical recovery
Data center DRAM (HBM + DDR5) represents fastest-growing segment with 60%+ gross margins

accomplishments: Shipped first HBM3E in volume to NVIDIA for the H100 program — first to market ahead of Samsung
HBM3E sold out through FY2026 — unprecedented visibility for a memory company
Opened new HBM fab capacity in Idaho (Boise) — $25B investment in US semiconductor manufacturing
Received CHIPS Act funding (~$6.1B) for domestic fab expansion
Launched LPDDR5X for next-gen AI edge devices — mobile and auto AI memory expansion

journey_52w.summary: Micron has been through a complete memory cycle: from trough EPS losses in 2023, through recovery, and now into the HBM supercycle. The current 6-contraction VCP is the deepest pause in that run — not a breakdown, but an accumulation zone before the next leg.

risks: EARNINGS IN 2 DAYS (June 25) — do not initiate new full positions; binary gap risk in both directions
SK Hynix market share battle for HBM — SK Hynix still has ~55% HBM market share vs Micron's ~20%
NAND oversupply risk — consumer PC/phone NAND pricing volatile and margins lower than HBM
Blackwell GPU production ramp: if NVIDIA delays, HBM demand shifts with it
catalysts: June 25 earnings — HBM3E ASP and allocation through 2026-2027 is the single most important metric
NVIDIA Blackwell ramp confirmation — each B200 GPU requires 192GB HBM3E (8 Micron stacks)
HBM4 qualification win — next-generation standard; whoever wins extends the cycle
CHIPS Act fab expansion — domestic HBM production creates supply chain security premium
watch: June 25 earnings — hold/reduce decision must be made before the report
Post-earnings: if guidance is raised, the $1,211 52w high breakout from this VCP would be very powerful
Post-earnings: if guidance disappoints, the VCP's $988 stop is the defense level
critical.valuation: fwd P/E 8.9x on EPS +756% is a flagrant mispricing — the market is still pricing Micron as a commodity memory company. HBM transforms this into a specialty memory business with pricing power, 70%+ gross margins on HBM, and sold-out capacity through 2026. Fair value on a 15-18x earnings basis implies 69-102% upside.

analyses.name: SpaceX Inc. (via SPCX vehicle)
analyses.verdict: SPCX is a newly listed vehicle giving public investors exposure to SpaceX — the most consequential private aerospace company on Earth. This analysis leads with the business thesis rather than quant signals because the stock is a new listing (is_new_listing flag) with no established scan metrics or TradingView rating. SpaceX is the world's dominant commercial launch provider (Falcon 9 has >90% global market share), the operator of the fastest-growing broadband network on Earth (Starlink, 6M+ subscribers), and the primary NASA and DOD contractor for crewed spaceflight, national security launches, and the Artemis lunar program. First Wall Street analyst initiations are occurring this week. This is a position-building opportunity at or near IPO prices for a generational platform company.

financials.summary: SpaceX does not report public financials. Estimates based on Bloomberg, Reuters, and analyst initiation reports indicate: Starlink revenue ~$11B+ annually (growing 80%+ YoY), Commercial Launch revenue ~$7B annually, Government contracts (NASA, DOD) ~$4B+ annually. Combined estimated revenue: $22B+ with estimated EBITDA margin of 25-30%. First Wall Street analyst initiations occurring this week.
highlights: Starlink: 6M+ active subscribers in 100+ countries — fastest broadband network growth in history
Falcon 9: 250+ consecutive successful launches — unprecedented reliability in commercial aerospace
Starship: first successful orbital test (2025); reusable 100+ ton to orbit changes economics of everything
DOD and NASA contracts: classified DOD launches + Artemis lunar program = $4B+ government revenue
First Wall Street analyst initiations occurring this week — institutional visibility increasing

accomplishments: Falcon 9 achieved 250+ consecutive successful launches — no other rocket has ever achieved this reliability
Starlink reached 6M+ paying subscribers across maritime, aviation, government, and residential segments
Starship completed first successful orbital flight and ocean landing — future of ultra-heavy reusable launch
SpaceX dominates US national security space launch with classified DOD missions
Polaris Dawn mission: first private spacewalk in history — demonstrating crew capability beyond ISS

journey_52w.summary: SPCX is a new listing — IPO price history is the only reference point. The stock's price discovery phase will be defined by analyst initiations, Starlink subscriber updates, and Starship commercial launch milestones.

Elon Musk key-person risk — SpaceX valuation partly reflects his vision and execution reputation
catalysts: Starship commercial launch program — first revenue-generating Starship launch would be a massive re-rating event
Starlink aviation expansion — every commercial aircraft adding Starlink is a high-ARPU customer
Direct-to-cell Starlink activation — T-Mobile partnership covers 90% of US geography

Analyst initiations this week — first price targets define the institutional value anchor

critical.valuation: SpaceX's private valuation was $350B+ in the most recent secondary share sale. Starlink alone at 6M subscribers growing 80%+ YoY could justify $200B+ standalone. Commercial launch is effectively a monopoly business at current costs. Starship's potential to reduce launch costs to $10/kg from $2,000/kg creates a platform that could enable entirely new space economy sectors. This is long-duration venture capital risk with public market liquidity — size accordingly and build the position over time.
